workflow_name: TEST - SOTA 2026 - WF2 Graph RAG V3.3 - CORRECTED

message.content:
Microsoft was founded by Bill Gates and Paul Allen on April 4, 1975. The two childhood friends had been fascinated by computers since their teenage years and shared a vision of putting a computer on every desk and in every home.

Gates and Allen met as students at Lakeside School in Seattle, where they bonded over their shared interest in programming. They started their first venture together in high school, creating a program called Traf-O-Data to analyze traffic patterns. Although this initial business was not successful, it provided valuable experience for their future endeavors.

In 1975, Allen showed Gates an article about the Altair 8800 microcomputer in Popular Electronics magazine. Recognizing the potential of personal computers, they contacted MITS, the company that produced the Altair, and offered to write a version of the BASIC programming language for the machine. This led to the official founding of Microsoft in Albuquerque, New Mexico, where MITS was headquartered.

Initially, Microsoft was a small company with just a few employees. Gates and Allen focused on developing software for various microcomputers. Their big break came in 1980 when IBM approached Microsoft to develop an operating system for their new personal computer. Microsoft purchased an operating system called 86-DOS from another company, modified it, and licensed it to IBM as PC-DOS. This deal catapulted Microsoft to the forefront of the rapidly growing personal computer industry.

Over the years, Microsoft expanded its product line and grew into one of the world's largest technology companies. Gates served as CEO until 2000 and remained chairman until 2014, while Allen left the company in 1983 due to health issues but remained on the board until 2000.

json.query:
MATCH (n)
WHERE ((n.name IN $entity_names
      OR ANY(ename IN $entity_names WHERE toLower(n.name) CONTAINS toLower(ename))
      OR ANY(ename IN $entity_names WHERE toLower(ename) CONTAINS toLower(n.name)))
      OR toLower(n.name) IN [x IN $entity_names | toLower(x)]
      OR ANY(ename IN $entity_names WHERE size(ename) > 3 AND toLower(n.name) CONTAINS toLower(ename)))
  AND (n.tenant_id = $tenant_id OR n.tenant_id IS NULL)
WITH n,
     CASE
       WHEN n:Organization THEN 1.3
       WHEN n:Person THEN 1.2
       WHEN n:City THEN 1.1
       WHEN n:Museum THEN 1.2
       WHEN n:Technology THEN 1.0
       WHEN n:Disease THEN 1.0
       ELSE 0.9
     END as entity_priority
ORDER BY entity_priority DESC
LIMIT 10

OPTIONAL MATCH path = (n)-[r*1..3]-(m)
WHERE (m.tenant_id = $tenant_id OR m.tenant_id IS NULL)
WITH path, n as startNode, entity_priority,
     reduce(score = entity_priority, rel IN relationships(path) |
       score + CASE type(rel)
         WHEN 'A_CREE' THEN 1.5
         WHEN 'CAUSE_PAR' THEN 1.4
         WHEN 'CONNECTE' THEN 1.3
         WHEN 'PROTEGE_CONTRE' THEN 1.3
         WHEN 'ETUDIE' THEN 1.2
         WHEN 'UTILISE' THEN 1.2
         WHEN 'CIBLE' THEN 1.2
         WHEN 'EXPOSE_A' THEN 1.1
         WHEN 'VISE_A_LIMITER' THEN 1.1
         WHEN 'SOUS_ENSEMBLE_DE' THEN 1.0
         WHEN 'ETEND' THEN 1.0
         ELSE 0.8
       END
     ) as path_score,
     length(path) as path_length

WHERE path_score > (path_length * 0.3)

RETURN
  startNode.name as start_entity,
  [node IN nodes(path) | {name: node.name, type: labels(node)[0]}] as path_nodes,
  [rel IN relationships(path) | type(rel)] as path_relations,
  path_score,
  path_length
ORDER BY path_score DESC
LIMIT $max_results
json.hyde_document:
Microsoft was founded by Bill Gates and Paul Allen on April 4, 1975. The two childhood friends had been fascinated by computers since their teenage years and shared a vision of putting a computer on every desk and in every home.

Gates and Allen met as students at Lakeside School in Seattle, where they bonded over their shared interest in programming. They started their first venture together in high school, creating a program called Traf-O-Data to analyze traffic patterns. Although this initial business was not successful, it provided valuable experience for their future endeavors.

In 1975, Allen showed Gates an article about the Altair 8800 microcomputer in Popular Electronics magazine. Recognizing the potential of personal computers, they contacted MITS, the company that produced the Altair, and offered to write a version of the BASIC programming language for the machine. This led to the official founding of Microsoft in Albuquerque, New Mexico, where MITS was headquartered.

Initially, Microsoft was a small company with just a few employees. Gates and Allen focused on developing software for various microcomputers. Their big break came in 1980 when IBM approached Microsoft to develop an operating system for their new personal computer. Microsoft purchased an operating system called 86-DOS from another company, modified it, and licensed it to IBM as PC-DOS. This deal catapulted Microsoft to the forefront of the rapidly growing personal computer industry.

Over the years, Microsoft expanded its product line and grew into one of the world's largest technology companies. Gates served as CEO until 2000 and remained chairman until 2014, while Allen left the company in 1983 due to health issues but remained on the board until 2000.

error.description:
Failed query: SELECT summary, relevance_score, entity_names 
FROM community_summaries 
WHERE entity_names && ARRAY[]::text[]
  AND tenant_id = ''
ORDER BY relevance_score DESC 
LIMIT 5
error.name: NodeOperationError

parameters.query:
=SELECT summary, relevance_score, entity_names 
FROM community_summaries 
WHERE entity_names && ARRAY[{{ $('Neo4j Query Builder (Deep Traversal V2)').json.entities.map(e => "'" + e.replace(/'/g, "''") + "'").join(',') }}]::text[]
  AND tenant_id = '{{ $('OTEL Init').json.user_context.tenant_id }}'
ORDER BY relevance_score DESC 
LIMIT 5

json.hyde_document:
Microsoft was founded by Bill Gates and Paul Allen on April 4, 1975. The two childhood friends had been fascinated by computers since their teenage years and shared a vision of putting a computer on every desk and in every home.

Gates and Allen met as students at Lakeside School in Seattle, where they bonded over their shared interest in programming. They started their first venture together in high school, creating a program called Traf-O-Data to analyze traffic patterns. Although this initial business was not successful, it provided valuable experience for their future endeavors.

In 1975, Allen showed Gates an article about the Altair 8800 microcomputer in Popular Electronics magazine. Recognizing the potential of personal computers, they contacted MITS, the company that produced the Altair, and offered to write a version of the BASIC programming language for the machine. This led to the official founding of Microsoft in Albuquerque, New Mexico, where MITS was headquartered.

Initially, Microsoft was a small company with just a few employees. Gates and Allen focused on developing software for various microcomputers. Their big break came in 1980 when IBM approached Microsoft to develop an operating system for their new personal computer. Microsoft purchased an operating system called 86-DOS from another company, modified it, and licensed it to IBM as PC-DOS. This deal catapulted Microsoft to the forefront of the rapidly growing personal computer industry.

Over the years, Microsoft expanded its product line and grew into one of the world's largest technology companies. Gates served as CEO until 2000 and remained chairman until 2014, while Allen left the company in 1983 due to health issues but remained on the board until 2000.
